ESCO2^R80S/R80S homozygous mutants exhibit developmental delay and die before hatching. Head deformities (particularly brain and eye malformation) and somite malformation are observed in homozygous mutants. In chromosomal analysis, mutants also show a significant increase in the chromosomal abnormalities, premature centromere separation.

詳細は Takashima et al.: Phenotype analysis of medaka mutant reveal the importance of bilaterally synchronized expression of isthmic fgf8 for bilaterally symmetric formation of the optic tectum. Genesis, 46, 537-545, 2008. を見よ。HO4C近交系の集団からみつかったもので、自然発生突然変異で生存可能である。表現型：stage 23/24の胚で、脳の中脳部（視蓋）の左右対称生が一過性に破れ、左右どちらかにかたよる。左右のどちらになるかはランダムである。遺伝様式：母性効果と浸透度のある優性と推定されている。

See Takashima et al.: Phenotype analysis of medaka mutant reveal the importance of bilaterally synchronized expression of isthmic fgf8 for bilaterally symmetric formation of the optic tectum. Genesis, 46, 537-545, 2008.A spontaneous viable mutation found in a population of HO4C inbred strain.Oot Phenotype: The hemilobes of the prospective tectum started to evaginate on both sides in the normal embryos at stage 23 (12-somite stage, 41 h after fertilization), resulting in the bilaterally symmetric formation of the tectum. However, in the Oot embryo, only one of the hemilobes was observed to evaginate normally, whereas the hemilobe on the other side enlarged in an inward direction rather than an outward direction, forming an asymmetrical tectum and a dented hemilobe. Consequently, both hemilobes inflated in the same direction on both sides. Among sibling mutant embryos, this unidirectional expansion occurred as frequently to the left as it did to the right. All other regions of the mutant embryos were normal. The mutant embryos were not associated with any situs inversus or unusual cardiac looping. The Oot phenotype was first detectable morphologically at stage 23/24 (from 12- to 16-somite stages) and was observable usually until the end of stage 24 (17-somite stage, 49 h after fertilization). At stage 25 (18- to 19-somite stage, 50 h after fertilization), the brain neurocoel enlarged in the normal embryos. Although about 10% of the mutant embryos persisted to exhibit the phenotype even up to the hatching stage (stage 39, 9days after fertilization), in most of them the dented hemilobes started to evaginate after stage 25, eventually forming tecta that exhibited almost normal symmetry. After this point, the Oot phenotype was usually hardly discernible.

Fish of this strain are homozygotes of three recessive body-color mutations; the bg8 mutation on the slc45a2 gene, the lf mutation on the leucophore free gene, and the gu mutation on the guanineless gene. One of the see-through strains.
Almost no scales, only a few, large scales.

Vacuolar-type H(+) ATPases (V-ATPases) are multimeric protein complexes that play a universal role in the acidification of intracellular compartments in eukaryotic cells. In a ENU screen, we have isolated the recessive medaka mutation tintachina (tch), which carries an inactivating modification of the conserved glycine residue (G75R) of the proton pump subunit atp6v1Ba/vatB1. Mutant embryos show normal morphology but penetrant pigmentation defects from stage 27 on. As development proceeds, embryos display progressive brain apoptosis and finally die before hatching.
Pair of iridocyte spots on brain membrane is absent.
Physiological color change of melanophores is slow.

This mutant is a leptin receptor-defficient medaka. This knockout mutant had a　point mutation of cysteine for stop codon at the 357th amino acid just before the leptin-binding domain. The evidence for loss of function of leptin-mediated signaling in the mutant is based on a lack of response to feeding in the expression of key appetite-related neuropeptides in the diencephalon.
